title: Anthephora acuminata
taxonomy: Anthephora acuminata is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it belongs to the family Poaceae. This classification is further broken down into the order Poales, which includes various grasses and sedge-like plants. The genus Anthephora is a group of annual grasses native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Poaceae family, Anthephora acuminata is a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Anthephora acuminata, also known as the Bushy Hair Grass, is a species of grass native to Africa and commonly used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Anthephora acuminata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
